role: Security Adviser to Prime Minister Mousavi (1982–1989); key Iranian negotiator in the Iran-Contra affair
bio: Mohsen Kangarloo served as security adviser to Prime Minister [Mir-Hossein Mousavi](/people/mir-hossein-mousavi) from 1982 to 1989 and was one of the key Iranian negotiators in the [Iran-Contra affair](/events/iran-contra-affair-1985). In February 1986, he met with Oliver North, [Manucher Ghorbanifar](/people/manucher-ghorbanifar), and Albert Hakim in Germany to discuss weapons transfers and hostage releases. Kangarloo pushed for the United States to provide Iran advanced air-to-air missiles for its fleet of F-14 fighters needed in the [Iran-Iraq War](/events/iran-iraq-war). He later stated in a 2018 interview that [Hezbollah](/orgs/hezbollah) took hostages 'because it can be useful for us,' confirming Iran's strategic leverage over the hostage situation. He died on 25 March 2020.
editorial_notes: Born 6 September 1947. Died 25 March 2020. Security adviser to PM Mousavi 1982–1989. Key negotiator at February 1986 Germany meeting with North/Ghorbanifar/Hakim.
